title: The mean-field theory of superfluid-superconducting vortex states in the outer core of neutron stars
abstract: Purpose: Characterize superfluid-superconducting vortex states at arbitrary pressures with $T_{cp}\neq T_{cn}$, assuming both proton and neutron mean-fields are formed by spin-0 Cooper pairs.   Method: The existing mean-field theory is extended to account for $T_{cp}\neq T_{cn}$. The pressure dependence of the pairing gap energy $Δ_{\alpha0}$ is quantitatively established on the basis of the effective chiral field theory. To link $T_{cα}$ with $Δ_{\alpha0}$, I use the weak-coupling result $T_{cα}\approx0.57Δ_{\alpha0}$. A quadratic scaled-temperature ($T/T_{cp}$) dependence of the thermodynamic magnetic field is postulated in analogy with pure superconductors. The $T/T_{cα}$-dependence of the gap $Δ_{αT}$ is inferred from the many-body approximations for the pure neutron matter.   Results: An empirical $T/T_{cα}$-dependence for the mean-field is constructed to account for the interplay between the condensation and the magnetic energies. The superfluid entrainment is found to increase the size of the vortex core and to decrease the effective magnetic penetration depth. The size of the neutron vortex core is found to be larger than the magnetic penetration depth in the outer core.   Conclusions: The usual approximation of infinitely thin vortex line (the London's approximation) for the neutron vortex is found to be irrelevant in the entire outer core and for the proton vortex is found to be limited to vicinity of the crust-core transition. The developed mean-field theory paves the way to study the vortex microscopic structure, the angular momentum, the magnetization and the vortex-fluxtube interaction energy.
summary: This paper develops an extended mean-field theory to describe the complex vortex structures in the superfluid-superconducting mixture of a neutron star's outer core. It specifically addresses the interplay between neutron and proton components through entrainment and temperature-dependent pairing gaps. The results show that standard approximations like the London model fail to accurately describe neutron vortices in this regime.
main_problem: The paper aims to characterize the quantum vortex states in the outer core of neutron stars by accounting for the pressure and temperature dependence of superfluid and superconducting parameters, specifically when critical temperatures for protons and neutrons are not equal.
main_result: The study finds that superfluid entrainment increases the neutron vortex core size and decreases the effective magnetic penetration depth, and demonstrates that the London approximation is invalid for neutron vortices throughout the outer core.
method: The author extends an existing mean-field theory using a Ginzburg-Landau-like framework, incorporating effective chiral field theory for density dependence and empirical temperature-dependent functions for the pairing gaps.
model_or_system: The system is the outer core of a neutron star, modeled as a mixture of superfluid neutrons and superconducting protons. The model uses an energy functional that includes magnetic energy, kinetic energies, condensation energy, and superfluid entrainment effects.
key_observables: Neutron and proton vortex core radii, effective magnetic penetration depth, pairing gap energy, and thermodynamic critical magnetic field.
important_parameters: Critical temperatures (Tcp, Tcn), pairing gaps (delta_alpha0), scaled temperature (tau_alpha), entrainment parameter (cnp), and baryon density (nb).
main_assumptions: The model assumes S-wave pairing for both species, neglects triplet neutron superfluidity (P-wave) effects, assumes the matter is isothermal, and assumes the total nucleon density remains fixed.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows critical temperatures as a function of baryon density; Figure 2 displays the scaled pairing gap for neutron matter; Figure 3 shows dimensionless coefficients and the thermodynamic critical field; Figure 4 compares various characteristic lengths like core radii and penetration depths.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the thermodynamic framework and energy functional, moves to establishing the pressure and temperature dependence of the pairing gaps, derives the dimensionless equations of motion for the vortex structure, and concludes with a numerical evaluation of characteristic lengths and the validity of the London approximation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2. Triangles: Dependence of the scaled pairing gap ∆(τα)/∆(0) in pure neutron matter corresponding to the weak-coupling (BCS) plus Hartree-Fock model, where τα corresponds to T/Tc, with Tc = 0.57∆(0) and ∆(T) adopted from Fig. 1 of [37] with the EM+3NF force. Dashed and dotted lines show the numerical fit corresponding to Eq. (34) with c1 = 1.4 and Eq. (35) with c2 = 1.5.

FIG. 1. Critical temperatures Tcα of neutron and proton pairing in the weak pairing approximation inferred from Eq. (33) and the data on the pairing gap energy at T = 0, ∆α0, reported in [2] and [3] on the basis of the effective chiral field theory. The vertical dashed line shows the critical baryon density, below which uniform nuclear mat- ter of the outer core is unstable with respect to density fluctuations and the inner crust region is expected.

FIG. 3. The dimensionless coefficients γ0, γ1, γ2, γ3, Eqs. (46)-(47), and the thermodynamic critical magnetic field, Eq. (41). Calcula- tions are done with T = 108 K. The data relevant to the outer core are located to the right from the blue dashed line.

FIG. 4. Characteristic lengths associated with the superfluid vortex state: the effective magnetostatic penetration depth λ eff T , Eq. (55), the core radius of purely neutron rnT , Eq. (51), or purely proton rpT , Eq. (53), vortex. For comparison, the coherence lengths of proton superconductor ξpT and of neutron superfluid ξnT are shown together with the intrinsic theoretical parameter λT , Eqs. (37)-(42). Calculations are done with T = 108 K. The data relevant to the outer core are located to the right from the blue dashed line.
